verifiedBehavior.fact: Folders have dedicated TileFolderViewModel and TileFolderRegionViewModel types; they are not ordinary adjacent tiles.

Creating a folder is a group-level rearrange branch gated by ITileViewModel::IsHostableByFolder. It creates a dedicated collection, moves the target and dragged tile models into it, and constructs a TileFolderViewModel at the resolved bounds.
Dragging onto an existing folder uses a separate add-to-folder path and records TileAddedToFolder activity.
Folder children are hosted by a dedicated folder region. Insert and remove operations change hosted state and emit element-change notifications.
An empty folder region is deleted. The inspected branch checks for zero children; no binary evidence was found for automatically dissolving a one-child folder.
The expanded folder is an inline folder region with its own nested tile panel, separators, and editable folder name.
Activating a folder toggles ITileFolderViewModel.IsExpanded directly; the folder does not open a separate window.

Expansion inserts a full-width folder-region placeholder at X=0 and Y=folder CellLocation.Y + CellSize.Y. Region bounds changes update the same placeholder, so later tiles are displaced without rewriting their persisted cell locations.
The expanded region is appended as a dedicated TileGrid element and receives its own bounds-change subscription. Collapse removes the region and defers unhosting through the region timer.
Folder child rearrangement is owned by TileFolderRegionViewModel, including move, insert, remove, rollback-related event handling, and deletion when the child collection reaches zero.

implementationContract.tileStartManualDissolvePlacement: Restore children to the containing group using folder origin plus folder-local child coordinates, then normalize collisions without discarding children.

unresolved: The exact native center-target tolerance beyond the verified bounds hit test.
The maximum folder child count, if any.

The exact duration and easing curve of the platform RepositionThemeTransition/ContentThemeTransition used during folder expansion and collapse; TileStart currently uses a separately testable WPF approximation while preserving the verified geometry and state transitions.

Whether the stock menu permits more than one expanded folder in every presentation mode; the inspected group model stores a vector of active folder regions and does not collapse another folder inside UpdateFolderExpanded.
